title: phoradendron villosum subsp. coryae
intro: Phoradendron villosum subsp. coryae is a species of mistletoe, a type of hemiparasitic plant that grows on the branches of its host tree, typically oak species. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which make it useful in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Phoradendron villosum subsp. coryae include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and improving respiratory health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional remedies, such as poultices and salves.
